---
title: 'Topic: No More Facebook... for 28 days or more?'
date: '2014-12-27T20:13:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6713752111584683285'
blogger_filename: '/2014/12/topic-no-more-facebook-for-28-days-or.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Technology & Tools'
  - 'Keys To Success'
  - 'News & Events'
---

<h3>I Deactivated Facebook... for 28 days.</h3><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-logo-square-webtreatsetc-17dfe3f25ab1.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-logo-square-webtreatsetc-17dfe3f25ab1.png" height="320" width="320" /></a></div><br /><h4>I'm not sure why yet.</h4><br />I wasn't using it much, and when I did I found it distracting, in a non-productive way. I left Twitter up for now, and Google+ is so pervasive that I'd have to delete ALL my Google activity/accounts in order to walk away from it. <br /><br />Deactivating is not the same as Deleting, that would be permanent. I set Facebook to auto-reactivate in 28 days. I want to run this as an experiment. Can I make it 28 days without Facebook? I think so. I may come back. I'm not sure yet.<br /><br />What could I accomplish in the next 28 days? Write a new book? Publish some new posts? Work overtime at my job? I don't know. I'd like this to be more purposeful. <br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><h1><span class="squares"></span><a href="http://www.convertunits.com/dates/28/daysfrom/Dec+27,+2014" target="_blank"><span style="font-size: small;"><span class="title_grn">28 days from Dec 27, 2014</span></span></a></h1><span style="font-size: small;"></span><span style="font-size: small;">Want to figure out the date that is exactly twenty-eight days from Dec 27, 2014 without counting?</span><br /><span style="font-size: small;"></span><span style="font-size: small;">Your starting date is December 27, 2014 so that means that 28 days later would be <b>January 24, 2015</b>.</span><br /><span style="font-size: small;"></span><span style="font-size: small;">You can check this by using the date difference calculator to measure the <a href="http://www.convertunits.com/dates/from/Dec+27,+2014/to/Jan+24,+2015">number of days from Dec 27, 2014 to Jan 24, 2015</a>.</span></blockquote><br /><h4>January 24, 2015</h4><br />What would I like to accomplish by January 24, 2015? I'd like to write one page/post or more on my <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/search/label/White%20Noise" target="_blank">AuthorFun Fiction blog</a>, every day, all 28 days. That starts today... so I guess I'd better cross today off my list? I'm going to focus on my White Noise tag, but I may do others. <br /><br /><br />You can see if I keep up with that <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/search/label/White%20Noise" target="_blank">HERE</a>. <br /><br /><br /><br />In other words... will I #<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>? <br /><br /><br />---<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>What do you think about this idea, would you take 28 days off of Facebook to do something productive with your time?</b></blockquote><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span><br /><br /><h4><div><script src="https://apis.google.com/js/plusone.js"></script><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></div></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a><br /><br /></div><div><h4><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">This Weeks Resource:</span></h4><div><ul><li><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/1595547924/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=1595547924&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkId=K2QBJGS34W2P36JZ">Circle Series 4-in-1 (The Circle Series)</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=1595547924" height="1" style="border: none !important; margin: 0px !important;" width="1" /></li></ul><br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="//ws-na.amazon-adsystem.com/widgets/q?ServiceVersion=20070822&amp;OneJS=1&amp;Operation=GetAdHtml&amp;MarketPlace=US&amp;source=ss&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;ad_type=product_link&amp;tracking_id=your0f4d-20&amp;marketplace=amazon&amp;region=US&amp;placement=1595547924&amp;asins=1595547924&amp;linkId=XEHSVCC2FEFT2OU2&amp;show_border=true&amp;link_opens_in_new_window=true" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe><br /><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i></span></div><div>- <br /><br /><!-- Begin MailChimp Signup Form --><link href="//cdn-images.mailchimp.com/embedcode/slim-081711.css" rel="stylesheet" type="text/css"></link><style type="text/css"> #mc_embed_signup{background:#fff; clear:left; font:14px Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;  width:200px;}  /* Add your own MailChimp form style overrides in your site stylesheet or in this style block.     We recommend moving this block and the preceding CSS link to the HEAD of your HTML file. */ </style><br /><div id="mc_embed_signup"><form action="//darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank"><label for="mce-EMAIL">Subscribe By Email</label> <input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" placeholder="email address" required="" type="email" /> <!-- real people should not fill this in and expect good things - do not remove this or risk form bot signups--><br /><div style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;"><input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" /></div><div class="clear"><input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div></form></div><!--End mc_embed_signup--><br /><br /><br /></div></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />
